subject: [PATCH v2 0/4] memcg: shrink obj_stock_pcp and cache multiple objcgs

reviews.author: Shakeel Butt (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged a specific concern about archs with 256KiB base page sizes and promised to fix it in v3.
reviews.raw_body:
Yes, I saw that comment. It is kind of very specific to archs with 256KiB base
page sizes. Anyways, I have a simple fix for that and there were minor
suggestions from others for simple changes, I will send v3 with the requested
changes.

2026-05-25.patch_summary: This patch series addresses a performance regression caused by the introduction of per-node obj_cgroup in commit 01b9da291c49. The regression occurs when threads of the same memcg run on different NUMA nodes, causing the per-CPU obj_stock_pcp to thrash. To fix this, the patches shrink the existing single-slot obj_stock_pcp fields and convert cached_objcg and nr_bytes into slot arrays using a multi-slot pattern already used by memcg_stock_pcp. This approach allows multiple objcgs per CPU to coexist in the stock without forcing a drain, making it more efficient than the temporary fix introduced earlier.
